framing: Architect at Gensler São Paulo applying to BIM technology lead role at construction tech startup in Mexico City

jd.title: BIM Technology Lead
jd.style_note: Startup-technical: specific BIM/AEC technology stack, integration-heavy, platform-focused

requirements.text: 6+ years of experience in architecture, structural engineering, or AEC technology with deep BIM expertise
Expert-level proficiency in Autodesk Revit and BIM 360 / Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC)
Experience with BIM standards: ISO 19650, LOD specifications (100-500), and model coordination workflows
Knowledge of IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) and openBIM data exchange formats
Programming skills for BIM automation: Dynamo (visual scripting), Revit API (C# or Python), or Grasshopper
Experience with construction project management platforms (Procore, PlanGrid, or Fieldwire)
Familiarity with GIS integration and digital twin platforms (Autodesk Tandem, Bentley iTwin, or Esri ArcGIS)

responsibilities: Lead BIM integration strategy for Procore's Latin America expansion
Develop technical workflows connecting Procore with Revit, Navisworks, and ACC for LATAM construction projects
Support enterprise AEC clients in Mexico, Brazil, and Colombia with BIM implementation and data migration
Build Revit API plugins and Dynamo scripts to automate data exchange between BIM models and Procore platform
Represent Procore at AEC industry events in Latin America (Expo CIHAC, Construtech LATAM)

expected_output.summary: Architect and BIM specialist with 8 years of experience at Gensler São Paulo, leading BIM implementation for commercial and mixed-use projects across Brazil. Expert in Autodesk Revit, Navisworks, and BIM 360. Developed Dynamo scripts automating quantity take-offs and clash detection workflows. Experience with ISO 19650 BIM standards on 3 LEED-certified projects. Registered architect (CAU/BR). Native Portuguese, fluent Spanish, business English. Seeking transition from design practice to AEC technology platform role.

experience.title: Senior BIM Manager
bullets: Led BIM implementation for 5 commercial tower projects in São Paulo (combined 250,000m² GFA) using Revit and BIM 360
Developed office-wide BIM Execution Plan (BEP) aligned with ISO 19650 information management standards
Created 30+ Dynamo scripts automating quantity take-offs, room data sheets, and drawing sheet generation
Managed model coordination and clash detection using Navisworks for multi-discipline coordination (architecture, structure, MEP)
Trained 25 architects and designers on Revit standards, family creation, and BIM workflows

experience.title: Architect / BIM Coordinator (promoted from Architect, 2020)
bullets: Coordinated BIM models for mixed-use development in Porto Alegre (LEED Gold, 45,000m²)
Developed Revit families and templates for Gensler São Paulo office standardisation
Managed IFC exports and openBIM data exchange with structural and MEP consultants using BIM Collab
Supported LOD 300-400 model development for construction documentation phase

certifications: CAU/BR — Registered Architect, Conselho de Arquitetura e Urbanismo do Brasil
education: Bacharelado em Arquitetura e Urbanismo — Universidade de São Paulo (FAU-USP) (2012-2017)

jd_requirements: 6+ years architecture structural AEC BIM expertise
Autodesk Revit BIM 360 Autodesk Construction Cloud ACC
Dynamo Revit API C# Python Grasshopper BIM automation
Procore PlanGrid Fieldwire construction project management
GIS digital twin Autodesk Tandem Bentley iTwin Esri ArcGIS
deliberate_flaws: No Revit API (C#) programming experience — only Dynamo visual scripting with basic Python nodes
No Grasshopper experience (Rhino ecosystem, not Revit)
No Procore, PlanGrid, or Fieldwire experience (used BIM 360 / ACC)
No GIS or digital twin platform experience (Autodesk Tandem, Bentley iTwin, ArcGIS)
No construction project management platform experience — architect, not construction manager
LOD experience tops at 400, not 500 (fabrication level)
No experience in Mexico or Colombia — only Brazil (different building codes, CICA standards)
No enterprise client support experience — internal BIM management, not client-facing platform support
Architecture practice to tech company transition — different product development cadence
No data migration experience (JD involves helping clients migrate to Procore)
Python skills are basic (Dynamo Python nodes only) — not production-grade scripting
